Steps.Message:
Sent by Theo at 22:36 - I'm pleased to confirm that all power in the Masefield Road area should be back on. The issue was caused by an underground network fault, which our engineers have made partial repairs to.

We know that a power cut can be difficult, and I'm sorry for the length of the time you were without your electricity. Please rest assured that our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our engineers are still working on repairs to our network.

With darkness now upon us we understand that it can be unsettling to have a power cut. It may help to locate a torch and anything else you may need so you can have it to hand just in case.

If you have any concerns please do not hesitate to contact our team, but we will work to fix the problem as quickly and safely as possible. Sent by Lisa at 21:22.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Lisa at 19:35 - We're sorry for the disruption to your power today, especially for those who may be more vulnerable without the electric. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. For more information just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR If you have any further concerns or questions though, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.

Steps.Message: All power in the area should be back on. We’re really sorry for any disruption this caused.

Our engineers are dealing with this and we estimate that most supplies will be restored by 01-MAR-2026 12:00.  Please accept our sincere apologies for any inconvenience this may cause.

Steps.Message:
We believe there may be an underlying issue causing the recent power cuts, but due to the nature of the electricity network, the heat created by a developing fault can cause a break in a cable to reseal itself. This can make it difficult to locate, so while our engineers are testing our equipment thoroughly to pinpoint the issue, this kind of problem may interrupt power multiple times before it's possible for us to fix. We are estimating to have power restored between 09:00-10:00.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

Sent by David at 07:23
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our engineers have now arrived on site in the Ladbroke Grove area.

As any issues that currently exist on our network have affected our equipment at the substation, engineers will start their testing there. They will then check our network equipment throughout the area to try and identify any problems, before trying to restore power.

If this is not successful it's possible that a cable repair may be needed. This can involve ground excavations, traffic management equipment or specialist teams.. As soon as our staff can safely update us, we'll give you the latest news. Sent by David at 07:11.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 06:13 - We're sorry your power has gone off again. We are sending our engineers back to the Ladbroke Grove area to investigate why.

At the moment we don't have an estimated timeframe until the engineers arrive and assess further.

We'll update you as soon as they have arrived on site and investigated.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hi there. We've just had a shift change, so David, Decasta, Roland and Aaron are now here to keep you updated with progress from our field teams.

We know it's frustrating being without power, but we wanted to assure you we're still here and as soon as we have some more information we'll update you again.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

If you want to speak to us in the meantime, you can contact us via Facebook or on Twitter @UKPowernetworks where the team I work on will be happy to help 24/7.
